Road Knights Motorcycle Club.description: The Road Knights Motorcycle Club is an outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1979 in New Zealand. It operates primarily in the South Island of New Zealand, with chapters also in Thailand, the Netherlands, and the United States. The club has been involved in violent conflicts with rival gangs such as the Damned and the Mongrel Mob. As of 2009, membership in New Zealand was low and former leadership had dispersed, died, or gone to jail.

descriptions: Hells Angels are allied with Road Knights, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: New Zealand's Hells Angels are allied with the Head Hunters and the Road Knights.

descriptions: Road Knights is listed as a rival of Mongrel Mob in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Power, Road Knights, Tribesmen, and Killer Beez

descriptions: Road Knights and Damned were in a war with violent incidents.
edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1980s, the Road Knights became involved in a war with the Damned which resulted in several violent incidents.

descriptions: Road Knights and Mongrel Mob are rivals, with a violent incident in 2008.
edges.evidence_quote: Its rivalry with the Mongrel Mob boiled over in June 2008 in Invercargill when the Road Knights' building was burned down and two motorcycles stolen.

descriptions: Members of both organizations participated together in the 1981 hunger strike.
edges.evidence_quote: seven IRA and three Irish National Liberation Army members starved themselves to death in pursuit of political status.

descriptions: The Di Lauro clan supported the De Gennaro clan in the faida against Ruocco.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Mugnano : Combattuta nei primi anni '90 tra il clan Ruocco e il rivale De Gennaro, supportato dai Di Lauro

descriptions: The Ruocco clan fought against the De Gennaro clan, which was supported by Di Lauro.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Mugnano : Combattuta nei primi anni '90 tra il clan Ruocco e il rivale De Gennaro, supportato dai Di Lauro

descriptions: Grim Reapers members murdered the president of the Outcasts Motorcycle Club.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1970, 11 members and 2 associates were sentenced to life in prison for the murder of Ronald Hartley, president of the Outcasts Motorcycle Club.

Clan dei marsigliesi.description: Italian-French criminal organization founded in Rome in 1973, operating between France and Italy during the early 1970s. Engaged in drug trafficking and lucrative kidnappings, becoming the first organized crime group to exert territorial control in Rome. It was composed of French ex-convicts from the Marseille milieu, linked to the French Connection.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra took over the heroin traffic previously controlled exclusively by the Clan des Marseillais.
edges.evidence_quote: Cosa nostra, sfruttando le direttrici tradizionali del contrabbando di sigarette, rilevò gran parte del traffico di eroina fino ad allora appannaggio esclusivo del clan dei marsigliesi

descriptions: The Clan dei Marsigliesi preceded the Banda della Magliana and changed the criminal landscape in Rome, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: con l'avvento del clan dei marsigliesi di Albert Bergamelli , Maffeo Bellicini e Jacques Berenguer trasferitisi nella capitale per dare vita a un redditizio business dello spaccio di eroina e, soprattutto, dei sequestri di persona, si determinò un deciso cambiamento dei rapporti di forze all'interno della piccola e frammentata malavita capitolina

descriptions: Camorra clans, allied with Sicilian mafiosi, defeated the foreign competition of the Clan des Marseillais.
edges.evidence_quote: i clan camorristici, in combutta con i mafiosi siciliani, riuscirono a sbaragliare la concorrenza straniera del clan dei marsigliesi

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana succeeded the Clan dei marsigliesi after its decline.
edges.evidence_quote: La successiva unione di alcune di queste batterie diede poi vita a una vera e propria banda per il controllo totale dei traffici illeciti romani conosciuta come Banda della Magliana .

descriptions: The Clan dei marsigliesi originated from members of the French Connection milieu.
edges.evidence_quote: provenienti in gran parte dal cosiddetto milieu marsigliese , il cartello criminale francese meglio conosciuto come " French Connection "

descriptions: Abate clan was an ally of Vollaro clan (now defunct).
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Ascione clan, Abate clan (defunct), Giuliano clan (defunct), Anastasio clan, Veneruso clan, Marfella clan

Bottoms Gang.description: The Bottoms Gang was an American street gang based in St. Louis, Missouri, active from around 1905 to 1917. Founded by Frank Hussey and Anthony Foley, the gang engaged in voter intimidation, armed robbery, assault, illegal lottery, and murder. Its members were primarily Irish-American, and the gang was known for extreme violence and feuding with the larger Egan's Rats gang and the St. Louis Police Department. The gang ceased to exist by the time the United States entered World War I.

descriptions: Surviving Bottoms Gang members joined the Chouteau Avenue Gang after the Bottoms Gang dissolved.
edges.evidence_quote: A handful of the surviving members had relocated to the Soulard district where they fell in with a local mob known as the Chouteau Avenue Gang.

descriptions: The Bottoms Gang feuded with Egan's Rats, their primary rival.
edges.evidence_quote: The number-one enemy of the Bottoms Gang was the larger, more powerful Egan's Rats gang.

descriptions: The Nixie Fighters were a splinter group that broke away from the Bottoms Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: A splinter group of the Bottoms Gang became known as the "Nixie Fighters" because of their penchant for fighting police officers.

descriptions: Egan's Rats and Bottoms Gang were rivals, with a war in 1916.
edges.evidence_quote: The Egan Gang's main rival at this time was the violent Bottoms Gang from the Twenty-second Ward.

descriptions: A member of the Madison crime family transferred to the Milwaukee family after the former disbanded.
edges.evidence_quote: John Candela — former member of the Madison crime family ; transferred to the Milwaukee family after the family disbanded.

Axe Gang.description: The Axe Gang was a militant organisation active in Shanghai and surrounding areas of the Republic of China during the early 20th century. Originating among Anhui migrant workers, it evolved from labour mobilisation into a faction involved in political violence and assassinations amid the Warlord Era and early Nationalist period. Its most prominent leader was Wang Yaqiao. The group declined after Wang's assassination in 1936.

descriptions: The Axe Gang formed connections with the New Guangxi clique for mutual benefit.
edges.evidence_quote: Wang's organisation maintained its relevance by forming connections with other regional military factions, including elements of the New Guangxi clique.

Individualists Tending to the Wild.description: Self-defined eco-extremist group that emerged in Mexico in 2011. It advocates for the destruction of technological civilization and has claimed responsibility for letter bombs and other attacks, though many claims have been disputed. The group draws inspiration from Ted Kaczynski and has attempted to extend its influence internationally, though its presence outside Mexico remains unconfirmed.

descriptions: Bandada Inquisidora Vengativa is a cell of ITS in Chile.
edges.evidence_quote: The cell “ Bandada Inquisidora Vengativa ”, another ITS group in Chile, took responsibility for an incendiary artifact

descriptions: Iconoclasta Sect is a cell of ITS that claimed a bomb in Athens.
edges.evidence_quote: The "Iconoclasta Sect" claimed responsibility for the attack

descriptions: Sociedad Salvaje Silvestre is a cell of ITS.
edges.evidence_quote: In a statement the self-styled "Sociedad Salvaje Silvestre" cell took responsibility for the attack

descriptions: Sureños Incivilizados is a cell of ITS in Chile.
edges.evidence_quote: The group "Sureños Incivilizados" made its appearance and totally burned a Transantiago bus

descriptions: ITS leader claimed influence from Tempel ov Blood.
edges.evidence_quote: A leader of the ITS claimed to have been influenced by the satanic fascist group the Tempel ov Blood .

descriptions: Black Fist MC is a support club of Trust MC in Romania.
edges.evidence_quote: There are also seven chapters in Romania along two support Black Fist chapters

descriptions: Los Lobos is allied with FARC 48th Front.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Los Tiguerones, Los Chone Killers, Kompania Bello, FARC 48th Front, Tren de Aragua

descriptions: Members of Lo Russo and Birra-Iacomino were arrested for planning an ambush together.
edges.evidence_quote: nove persone, quattro del clan Lo Russo di Miano e cinque dei Birra-Iacomino, vennero arrestate in quanto ritenute coinvolte nella pianificazione e nell'esecuzione dell'imboscata di Mario Ascione

descriptions: The Giuliano clan fought wars against the Lo Russo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Le numerose guerre tra gli anni ottanta e novanta contro i Contini , i Licciardi , i Lo Russo , i Misso e i Mazzarella depotenziano però notevolmente il clan Giuliano

Osmanen Germania.description: Turkish-nationalistic and extreme right criminal gang in Germany, formed between late 2014 and April 2015. It was involved in organized crime including assault, blackmail, prostitution, kidnapping, narcotics, and gun crime. The gang had connections to the Turkish ruling party AKP and the neo-fascist Grey Wolves. It was banned by the German Federal Ministry of the Interior in 2018.

descriptions: Osmanen Germania BC worked as security guards for the Grey Wolves from 2014 to 2018.
edges.evidence_quote: From 2014-2018 the Osmanen Germania were an extreme right criminal gang working as security guards for the gray wolves.

descriptions: Osmanen Germania had violent clashes with Bahoz.
edges.evidence_quote: There were also numerous violent clashes with the Kurdish-rooted Bahoz

descriptions: Osmanen Germania had connections with the Grey Wolves.
edges.evidence_quote: It had connections to the neo-fascist Grey Wolves .

descriptions: Milazzo and Magaddino were Castellammarese leaders who supported each other's families.
edges.evidence_quote: Gaspar Milazzo and his longtime ally, Buffalo Mafia boss Stefano Magaddino had secretly, but willingly supported their allies in New York and Chicago as they opposed Joe Masseria and his supporters.

descriptions: Castellammarese leaders supported Chicago Mafia boss Giuseppe Aiello against Al Capone.
edges.evidence_quote: Both men openly supported their allies, including Chicago Mafia boss Giuseppe Aiello, who was being opposed by a powerful Masseria ally in Chicago, Al Capone.

descriptions: Mario Donato Ferrazzo made agreements with clans from Crotone and Lombardy.
edges.evidence_quote: sarebbero stati realizzati accordi con clan del Crotonese e della Lombardia

descriptions: Brew Crew is an offshoot gang of the Zulu Warriors.
edges.evidence_quote: The Zulu Warriors have also seen offshoot gangs created such as the Brew Crew and the Junior Business Boys

descriptions: Kyoyu-kai was a constituent group within Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi yakuza organization included ... Kyoyu-kai

descriptions: Kyoyu-kai broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: powerful factions, including the Kobe-based Yamaken-gumi, the Osaka-based Takumi-gumi, and the Kyoyu-kai, broke away from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed a new group called the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi

Hudson Dusters.description: The Hudson Dusters were a New York City street gang formed in the late 1890s by Circular Jack, Kid Yorke, and Goo Goo Knox. Based in Manhattan's West Side, they controlled the Hudson River docks and engaged in burglary, armed robbery, and narcotics. The gang had ties to Tammany Hall and was known for cocaine-fueled violence. They were active until around 1917.

descriptions: The Boodle Gang fared poorly in gang wars against the Hudson Dusters, who dominated the area by the end of the 1890s.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Potashes , Hudson Dusters

descriptions: Hudson Dusters displaced Boodle Gang from dominance in Lower West Side Manhattan.
edges.evidence_quote: They and the much older Boodle Gang dominated Lower West Side Manhattan until the end of the 1890s when they were displaced by the then emerging Hudson Dusters

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters.
edges.evidence_quote: The Five Points Gang (led by Paul Kelly) would rise to prominence during the early 1900s, strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters, the Gopher Gang, and others during the period.

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Hudson Dusters

descriptions: The Hudson Dusters were rivals of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hudson Dusters

descriptions: Hudson Dusters fought against the Boodle Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the two gangs later became allies during the gang wars against Gay Nineties gangs, the Potashes and Boodle Gangs

descriptions: Infobox lists Boodle Gang as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Boodle Gang

descriptions: Hudson Dusters battled the Fashion Plates for control of docks.
edges.evidence_quote: it constantly battled smaller rival gangs including the Fashion Plates, the Pearl Buttons, and the Marginals for control of the Hudson River docks throughout the 1900s

descriptions: Infobox lists Fashion Plates as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Fashion Plates

descriptions: Infobox lists Gopher Gang as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Gopher Gang

descriptions: Hudson Dusters and Gopher Gang became allies during gang wars.
edges.evidence_quote: Knox, a former member of the Gopher Gang, had fled after a failed attempt to gain leadership of the gang from then-leader Marty Brennan. However, the two gangs later became allies during the gang wars against Gay Nineties gangs, the Potashes and Boodle Gangs

descriptions: Hudson Dusters battled the Marginals for control of docks.
edges.evidence_quote: it constantly battled smaller rival gangs including the Fashion Plates, the Pearl Buttons, and the Marginals for control of the Hudson River docks throughout the 1900s

descriptions: Marginals drove Hudson Dusters from their territory in 1914.
edges.evidence_quote: By 1914 however, with most of its leaders in jail or dead from drug overdoses, the remainder of the gang were driven from their territory by the Marginals under Tanner Smith

descriptions: Infobox lists Marginals as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Marginals

descriptions: Hudson Dusters battled the Pearl Buttons for control of docks.
edges.evidence_quote: it constantly battled smaller rival gangs including the Fashion Plates, the Pearl Buttons, and the Marginals for control of the Hudson River docks throughout the 1900s

descriptions: Infobox lists Pearl Buttons as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Pearl Buttons

descriptions: Hudson Dusters fought against the Potashes.
edges.evidence_quote: the two gangs later became allies during the gang wars against Gay Nineties gangs, the Potashes and Boodle Gangs

descriptions: Marginals member pursued a Hudson Dusters member with a gun, showing conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Smith was witnessed pursuing a member of the Hudson Dusters with a revolver in his hand

descriptions: Marginals succeeded Hudson Dusters in territory, indicating a structural succession.
edges.evidence_quote: succeeded the longtime Hudson Dusters in their territory of New York's Lower West Side

descriptions: The text states Neighbors' Sons were rivals of the Hudson Dusters.
edges.evidence_quote: The Neighbors' Sons was a New York street gang which, operating in the neighborhoods between Bleecker and Grove Streets , were rivals of the Gopher Gang and the Hudson Dusters during the early 1900s.

descriptions: Hudson Dusters are listed as rivals of Potashes in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Boodle Gang , Hudson Dusters

descriptions: The Misso and Torino clans were in conflict in the Sanità area.
edges.evidence_quote: seconda faida della Sanità : combattuta dal 2005 al 2007 tra il clan Misso e la fazione scissionista dei Torino, appoggiati dai Lo Russo di Miano.

descriptions: The Torino faction was supported by the Lo Russo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: appoggiati dai Lo Russo di Miano

descriptions: APC created self-defense forces in response to OPC's militarization, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: La APC tiene un lema 'para preservar la indivisibilidad del país'. El grupo creó unas autodefensas en respuesta a la militarización de los yorubas del OPC.

descriptions: Vietnamese Boyz is listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bloods, East Side Longos, Smiley Dragon, Tiny Rascal Gang, Crazy Brother Clan, Vietnamese Boyz, Wah Ching, Fresno Bulldogs

descriptions: Asian Boyz aligned with Vietnamese Boyz against Wah Ching.
edges.evidence_quote: the Asian Boyz (ABZ), who also aligned themselves with the Vietnamese Boyz (VBZ) in opposition to Wah Ching.

descriptions: Vietnamese Boyz Gang is listed as an ally of the Black Dragons in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Wah Ching , Vietnamese Boyz Gang, Oriental Lazy Boyz , Asian Boyz , Hop Sing Tong, United Bamboo Triad, 14k Triad

descriptions: M-19 was allied with MRTA.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Túpac Amaru Revolutionary Movement

descriptions: Shining Path fought against MRTA.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shining Path began to fight against Peru's other major guerrilla group, the Túpac Amaru Revolutionary Movement (MRTA)

descriptions: Infobox lists EPR as an enemy of Guerreros Unidos.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Gobierno de México, Ejército Mexicano, Armada de México, Cártel de Sinaloa, Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación, Cartel del Golfo, La Familia Michoacana, Los Viagras, EPR.

descriptions: Los Zetas considered the EPR an enemy according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Cártel de Sinaloa , Cártel del Golfo , Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación , Caballeros Templarios , Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima , EPR

descriptions: Avignone and Fazzalari are part of the same criminal alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 12 dicembre 2017 si conclude l'operazione Terramara-Closed ... presunte affiliate ai Avignone -Zagari-Fazzalari-Viola

descriptions: Viola and Zagari were in a violent feud with Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 2 luglio 1989 con l'uccisione di Rocco Neri scoppia una violenta faida tra gli Asciutto-Neri-Grimaldi e gli Zagari-Viola.

descriptions: Zagari and Fazzalari are part of the same criminal alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 12 dicembre 2017 si conclude l'operazione Terramara-Closed ... presunte affiliate ai Avignone -Zagari-Fazzalari-Viola

descriptions: Zagari is allied with Viola.
edges.evidence_quote: È alleata dei Viola , anch'essi di Taurianova .

descriptions: The Greco clan sought mediation from the Villabate family boss Antonio Cottone to end their internal feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Piddu the lieutenant asked for mediation from Antonio Cottone, the boss of the Mafia family of Villabate

descriptions: The Birmingham Boys lost control of racecourses to the Sabini gang, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: They lost control of the South East racecourses to the Sabini gang.

descriptions: The Sabini gang moved in on the Birmingham Boys gang.
edges.evidence_quote: With the Blinders' withdrawal from the criminal underworld, the Sabini gang moved in on the Birmingham Boys gang and solidified political control over Central England in the 1930s.

descriptions: Elephant and Castle Mob was a rival of the Sabini gang.
edges.evidence_quote: A rival of mobster Charles 'Darby' Sabini along with the Birmingham Boys and the Cortesi brothers of Saffron Hill

descriptions: Billy Hill's organisation succeeded Sabinis after the White family.
edges.evidence_quote: his empire was taken over by the White family led by Alf White and subsequently by the organisations of Jack Spot and Billy Hill

descriptions: Sabinis and Birmingham Boys were rival gangs that engaged in violent conflict over racecourse protection rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: The protection rackets proved to be extremely profitable and drew attention from other gangs such as Billy Kimber's Birmingham Boys . Violence between the groups increased, but the Birmingham boys were forced to vacate their claims when 23 of their number were locked up following the " Epsom Road Battle ".

descriptions: Cortesi brothers challenged Sabini's power, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Sabini managed to fend off challenges from rival gangs such as the Cortesi brothers from Saffron Hill

descriptions: Hoxton mob harassed Sabini's businesses, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: his businesses were routinely harassed by street gangs such as the Hoxton mob

descriptions: Jack Spot's organisation succeeded Sabinis after the White family.
edges.evidence_quote: his empire was taken over by the White family led by Alf White and subsequently by the organisations of Jack Spot and Billy Hill

descriptions: White family succeeded Sabinis after WWII, taking over their criminal empire.
edges.evidence_quote: his empire was taken over by the White family led by Alf White

descriptions: Pemotong Leher was one of the small units that took allegiance to SP88.
edges.evidence_quote: These small units took up names such as Pasukan Siluman (Ghost Squad), Srigala Hitam (Black Jackal), Pemotong Leher (Neck Cutters) and Garuda Putih (White Eagle).

descriptions: The Westies were rivals with Mickey Spillane's gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mickey Spillane 's gang

descriptions: The La Torre clan is a subgroup of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Camorra's organizational structure is divided into individual groups called 'clans'.

descriptions: La Torre clan is a Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: He is the brother of Camorra boss Augusto La Torre of the La Torre clan which had its base in Mondragone , Caserta .

descriptions: The La Torre clan is listed as a rival of the Casalesi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: La Torre clan (defunct)

descriptions: La Torre clan and Barbaro 'ndrina cooperated in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tamanaco operation that ended on 22 June 2010, destroyed a drug trafficking operation managed by the La Torre clan from Mondragone and the Barbaro 'ndrina from Platì .

descriptions: CGM is listed as a rival of Mali Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Beaumont Crew / Let’s Get Rich (LGR), Priory Court (PC) / Higham Hill, CGM, Anyone Can Go (ACG), Custom House (CH), Peckwater (51st)

descriptions: The Looney gang and William Gabel's gang were at war.
edges.evidence_quote: The Looney gang entered into a war with William Gabel's gang.

descriptions: Outlaws and Avenger members were together in a fight, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: On February 24, 2001, several members of the Dayton Outlaws chapter, along with members of the Avenger Motorcycle Club, visited Spanky's Dollhouse...

'Ndrina Dragone.description: The 'Ndrina Dragone is a 'ndrina (clan) of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta, originating from Cutro, Italy. Founded by Antonio Dragone in the 1960s, it engaged in extortion, robbery, and murder. The clan was involved in a long-running feud with the Oliverio family and later with the Grande Aracri clan. It established operations in Emilia-Romagna, particularly in Reggio Emilia, Modena, and Piacenza. The clan's power declined after the killing of Antonio Dragone in 2004 and subsequent police operations.

descriptions: Dragone is listed as an ally of Arena in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Carpino, Costanzo, Dragone, Gaglianesi, Mannolo, Trapasso

descriptions: Dragone is allied with Arena.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Dragone è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese originaria di Cutro alleata con i Mannolo di San Leonardo di Cutro e gli Arena di Isola Capo Rizzuto e gli Arabia di Cutro.

descriptions: Dragone and Grande Aracri were in conflict in the Cutro feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Cutro: combattuta tra la fine degli anni '90 e i primi del 2000, ha visto scontrarsi i Dragone e i Grande Aracri.

descriptions: Dragone and Grande Aracri were in a feud in 2004.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 10 maggio 2004 viene ucciso Antonio Dragone e in seguito il suo genero Gaetano Ciampà . La faida riprende nel 2004 con l'omicidio di Franco Arena scoppia una faida fra gli Arena -Dragone e i Nicoscia - Grande Aracri.

descriptions: Dragone is allied with Mannolo.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Dragone è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese originaria di Cutro alleata con i Mannolo di San Leonardo di Cutro e gli Arena di Isola Capo Rizzuto e gli Arabia di Cutro.

descriptions: Dragone lists Trapasso-Zoffreo as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Trapasso-Zoffreo

descriptions: Dragone is allied with Arabia.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Dragone è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese originaria di Cutro alleata con i Mannolo di San Leonardo di Cutro e gli Arena di Isola Capo Rizzuto e gli Arabia di Cutro.

descriptions: Dragone and Oliverio were in a feud in 1972.
edges.evidence_quote: A Cutro oltre ai Dragone vi erano gli Oliverio che tentavano di prendere il monopolio criminale della città fino allo scoppio di una faida nel 1972 che portò all'omicidio di: Francesco Dragone, Antonio Spagnolo, Vittorio Colacino e Gaetano, Domenico e Salvatore Oliverio.

descriptions: Dragone and Vasapollo were in a feud between 1999 and 2004.
edges.evidence_quote: Ne scoppia una anche tra i Dragone e i Vasapollo.

descriptions: Grande Aracri was in a feud (faida) with the Dragone family.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1999 scoppia la faida con l'omicidio di Raffaele Dragone

descriptions: Grande Aracri split from Dragone, resulting in a violent feud.
edges.evidence_quote: A violent split from the Dragone 'ndrina led to multiple murders and a long-lasting internal feud.

Los Rastrojos.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary group formed by dissidents of the AUC after its demobilization in 2006. It became independent following the murder of its founder in Venezuela in 2008 and was one of Colombia's most important drug trafficking organizations. The group finances itself through cocaine, marijuana, and heroin trafficking, as well as illegal gold mining. It operated mainly in Valle del Cauca and Cali, with influence extending to other parts of Colombia and western Venezuela. By 2017, the group had fragmented into smaller factions.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos is listed as a dominant paramilitary group alongside AUC successor groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2010s, the Black Eagles, Los Rastrojos, Los Urabeños, Los Paisas, Los Machos, Renacer, Los Gaitanistas, Nueva Generación, Bloque Meta, Libertadores del Vichada, the ERPAC, and The Office of Envigado comprised the dominant criminal and paramilitary organizations.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos was formed by Wilber Varela, a capo of the Norte del Valle Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos is a Colombian drug trafficking paramilitary organization . The group was formed by Norte del Valle cartel capo Wilber Varela, alias 'Jabon' and one of his right-hand men, 'Diego Rastrojo', around 2004 when Varela fell out with fellow-capo Diego Leon Montoya, alias ' Don Diego '.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos was a faction of the Cartel del Norte del Valle led by Wilber Alirio Varela.
edges.evidence_quote: Wilber Alirio Varela con sus aliados y su ejército personal que denominó " Los Rastrojos ".

descriptions: AGC declared war on Los Rastrojos for drug trade control.
edges.evidence_quote: In late 2011, the AGC declared war on Los Rastrojos over the control of the drug trade in Medellín.

descriptions: The text describes them as the two main groups competing for territorial control.
edges.evidence_quote: El Clan Úsuga y Los Rastrojos eran reconocidas como las dos grandes agrupaciones que han logrado una expansión territorial considerable...

descriptions: Clan del Golfo had a strong rivalry with Los Rastrojos.
edges.evidence_quote: La agrupación mantenía una fuerte rivalidad con otras bandas emergentes como Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Clan del Golfo had a strong rivalry with Los Rastrojos over territory and drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: La agrupación mantenía una fuerte rivalidad con otras bandas emergentes entre las que figuran Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Los Rastrojos is described as a rival criminal group, and a former member became leader of Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: un exmiembro del grupo criminal rival Los Rastrojos Marlon Gregorio Celis Caballero, alias 'Loquillo' o 'Felipe ', fue nombrado “como el nuevo líder del Clan del Golfo”

descriptions: They dispute drug trafficking and extortion territories.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos , uno de los grupos mafiosos que disputa el tráfico ilegal de drogas y ejecuta extorsiones en gran parte de los departamentos de Colombia

descriptions: Los Rastrojos is listed as an ally of AGC in infobox, despite earlier conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel , CJNG , Brothers Keepers , 'Ndrangheta , Sicilian Mafia , Los Choneros , Los Contrabandistas , Los Cachiros , Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Clan del Golfo and Los Rastrojos held a meeting in 2012 to agree on territory division and future alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: representantes del Clan Úsuga, al igual que de los desaparecidos Rastrojos y Paisas, habrían sostenido una reunión clandestina en 2012 en Medellín , para acordar una repartición de 'territorios' a nivel nacional y así acabar con los enfrentamientos entre las mismas organizaciones criminales y comenzar a trabajar en conjunto para una futura alianza.

descriptions: Factions of Los Rastrojos have strategically allied with Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: varias facciones de otros grupos narcoparamilitares se les han aliado estratégicamente, debido al debilitamiento territorial y militar de organizaciones como Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Both operate in the same drug trafficking arena.
edges.evidence_quote: Comparte el escenario del narcotráfico con otras bandas narcoterroristas , entre las que se encuentran Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Clan del Golfo bought a franchise from Los Rastrojos for extortion operations.
time_periods: finales del mes de noviembre de 2012
edges.evidence_quote: la bacrim realizó un millonario negocio con Los Rastrojos , luego de que el Clan Úsuga desembolsara $6.000.000.000 por una llamada «franquicia»

descriptions: Clan del Golfo and Los Rastrojos participated in a meeting to agree on territory division and future alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: representantes de Los Paisas , junto a los líderes del Clan Úsuga y Los Rastrojos , habrían sostenido una reunión clandestina en la ciudad de Medellín, con el fin de acordar una repartición de "territorios" ... para acabar con los enfrentamientos entre las mismas organizaciones criminales y comenzar a trabajar en conjunto para una futura alianza.

descriptions: A former member of Los Rastrojos became the leader of the Gulf Clan, indicating a transfer of personnel between the groups.
edges.evidence_quote: shortly before the arrest of Téllez, a former member of the rival Los Rastrojos criminal group Marlon Gregorio Celis Caballero, alias 'Loquillo' or 'Felipe', was named 'as the new leader of the Gulf Clan.'

descriptions: Clan del Golfo met with Los Rastrojos and Los Paisas in 2012 to agree on territory division and end conflicts.
edges.evidence_quote: representantes del Clan Úsuga, al igual que de los desaparecidos Rastrojos y Paisas, habrían sostenido una reunión clandestina en 2012 en la ciudad de Medellín , para acordar una repartición de "territorios" a nivel nacional y así acabar con los enfrentamientos

edges.source: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
descriptions: Los Rastrojos bought the Bloque Meta franchise from ERPAC in 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos habrían comprado en 2013 la franquicia del Bloque Meta

descriptions: Cachiros is allied with Los Rastrojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Los Rastrojos , Norte del Valle Cartel , Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Los Cachiros are allied with Los Rastrojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Sinaloa , Los Rastrojos , Cartel del Norte del Valle

descriptions: Los Paisas had Los Rastrojos as allies according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Los Paisas, Clan del Golfo, and Los Rastrojos held a meeting to agree on territory division and future alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: representantes de Los Paisas , junto a los líderes del Clan Úsuga y Los Rastrojos , habrían sostenido una reunión clandestina en la ciudad de Medellín, con el fin de acordar una repartición de "territorios" ... para acabar con los enfrentamientos entre las mismas organizaciones criminales y comenzar a trabajar en conjunto para una futura alianza.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos members conspired with AUC commander Macaco to murder Wilber Varela.
edges.evidence_quote: Authorities now believe Wilber Varela's main captains Diego Perez Henao alias 'Diego Rastrojo' and Luis Enrique Calle alias 'Combatiente' ('Combatant') conspired with AUC commander Carlos Mario Jiménez alias 'Macaco' to murder Varela.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos were formed by dissidents of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Fue compuesta por disidentes de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Los Rastrojos was formed by dissidents of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Fue compuesta por disidentes de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: Barrera Cartel is listed as an ally of Los Rastrojos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel, Barrera cartel, The Office of Envigado

descriptions: The founder of Los Rastrojos was linked to the Norte del Valle Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: por vincularlo a los negocios del Cartel del Norte del Valle , ser uno de los principales traficantes de drogas y ser el fundador de Los Rastrojos.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos originated as a splinter from the Norte del Valle Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rastrojos were born out of the powerful Norte del Valle drug cartel

descriptions: Los Rastrojos originated from the Norte del Valle cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos nacieron del poderoso cartel de la droga del Norte del Valle

descriptions: Clan del Golfo is listed as a rival of Los Rastrojos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Clan del Golfo, ELN

descriptions: Los Rastrojos were at war with Clan del Golfo from 2006 to 2012.
edges.evidence_quote: Mantuvieron una guerra con el Clan del Golfo entre 2006 y 2012.

descriptions: Members of Los Rastrojos defected to the Clan del Golfo, indicating a cooperative transfer of personnel.
edges.evidence_quote: las deserciones de sus miembros a su otrora rival "Clan del Golfo"

descriptions: Los Rastrojos and Clan del Golfo met to negotiate territory division and form an alliance, but it did not materialize.
edges.evidence_quote: representantes de Los Rastrojos , junto a los líderes del Clan del Golfo y los desaparecidos Paisas , sostuvieron una reunión clandestina en la ciudad de Medellín , para acordar una repartición de "territorios" a nivel nacional; de esta manera, buscaban acabar los enfrentamientos entre las mismas organizaciones criminales y trabajar en conjunto para una alianza que al final no se dio.

edges.target: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
descriptions: Los Rastrojos bought the franchise of the Meta Bloc from ERPAC in 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos habrían comprado en 2013 la franquicia del Bloque Meta, adscrita al Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia (ERPAC)

descriptions: Los Rastrojos are allied with Los Cachiros.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Los Cachiros

descriptions: Los Rastrojos and Los Machos were in constant war.
edges.evidence_quote: los cuales se mantuvieron en guerra constante debido a la enemistad entre 'Jabón' y 'Don Diego'

descriptions: Los Rastrojos were allied with Los Paisas (now dismantled).
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Los Paisas (desarticulado)

descriptions: Los Rastrojos and Los Paisas met to negotiate territory division and form an alliance, but it did not materialize.
edges.evidence_quote: representantes de Los Rastrojos , junto a los líderes del Clan del Golfo y los desaparecidos Paisas , sostuvieron una reunión clandestina en la ciudad de Medellín , para acordar una repartición de "territorios" a nivel nacional; de esta manera, buscaban acabar los enfrentamientos entre las mismas organizaciones criminales y trabajar en conjunto para una alianza que al final no se dio.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos entered into armed conflict with the ELN after the alliance ended.
edges.evidence_quote: By March 2020, Los Rastrojos no longer had an alliance with the Colombian left-wing militant group National Liberation Army (ELN) and was now involved in a direct armed conflict with the group as well.

descriptions: By March 2020, Los Rastrojos were in conflict with ELN.
edges.evidence_quote: el grupo ahora estaba en conflicto con el ELN luego de que los cuerpos de ocho miembros de Los Rastrojos fueran encontrados muertos

descriptions: Los Rastrojos had a cooperative agreement with the ELN for several years.
edges.evidence_quote: Currently, the Rastrojos have an agreement with the National Liberation Army (Ejército de Liberación Nacional – ELN) for several years in the departments of Cauca and Nariño.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos had an agreement with ELN for several years.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos tienen un convenio con el Ejército de Liberación Nacional (ELN) por varios años

descriptions: The Office of Envigado is listed as an ally of Los Rastrojos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel, Barrera cartel, The Office of Envigado

descriptions: Los Rastrojos are in territorial war with EPL dissidents.
edges.evidence_quote: guerra territorial que hay entre lo que queda de la disidencia del EPL y los llamados Rastrojos contra la guerrilla del ELN

descriptions: Los Rastrojos are allied with Los Pelusos.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Los Pelusos

descriptions: Los Rastrojos frequently fought battles against FARC.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos han librado frecuentemente batallas contra los grupos guerrilleros de las Fuerzas Armadas Revolucionarias de Colombia

descriptions: Los Rastrojos had a cooperative agreement with the FARC.
edges.evidence_quote: More recently they obtained a similar agreement with the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Fuerzas Armadas Revolucionarias de Colombia – FARC) in other parts of the country.

descriptions: Sinaloa Cartel is listed as an ally of Los Rastrojos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel, Barrera cartel, The Office of Envigado

descriptions: Los Zetas teamed up with Los Rastrojos to control drug trafficking routes.
edges.evidence_quote: Early in 2012 it was reported that Los Zetas were operating in the northern Venezuela – Colombia border, and had teamed up with the Colombian outfit called Los Rastrojos.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado has alliances with Los Rastrojos.
edges.evidence_quote: it has alliances with Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado had alliances with Los Rastrojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Mantenían alianzas con Los Rastrojos (actualmente desarticulados)

descriptions: Remnants of Los Rastrojos allied with EPL to form a new criminal structure.
edges.evidence_quote: un reducto de Los Rastrojos en el Catatumbo se habría aliado con el EPL para conformar una nueva estructura criminal

descriptions: EPL allied with Los Rastrojos to fight ELN.
edges.evidence_quote: quienes presuntamente habrían hecho una alianza con lo que quedó del grupo narcoparamilitar Los Rastrojos para combatir al ELN

descriptions: Los Rastrojos is an ally of Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: international alliances are thought to include ... Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Los Rastrojos is listed as a group under Black Eagles.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups - Los Rastrojos: operating in Cauca and Valle del Cauca (approximately 1,200 members).

descriptions: Fifth kumicho Koji Nakata was kaicho of Fifth Kenryu-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: He was kaicho of the Fifth Kenryu-kai .

descriptions: The Hornec brothers took over Claude Genova's operations after his death; Genova had taken over from the Zemmour brothers.
edges.evidence_quote: Claude Genova's connections and infrastructure was overtaken by the Hornec brothers in 1994.
